channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: EverQuest II Announces Launch Details for Next TLE, Wuoshi
item.content:encoded:
Details for EverQuest II’s next Time Locked Expansion server, Wuoshi, are here, ahead of its opening in June. 

The server opens on June 9th at noon PT to All Access members. Wuoshi was mentioned in last month’s Producer’s Letter along with a few details, but along with an overview, there’s a FAQ addressing questions that came up since that letter was shared. Also in that April Producer’s Letter was a cryptic first hint at the next expansion, but that announcement is still some time away. 

Wuoshi will be a standard TLE with free trade and without PvP, starting with all expansions up to Echoes of Faydwer unlocked. Expansions will unlock every three months, ending with Chaos Descending. The expanded start means that classes available through Echoes of Faydwer will be available from Wuoshi’s opening, with others unlocking with their expansions.

The expanded start also means that nearly all live events in 2026 after the server launch can happen without modifications, except Tinkerfest. Another modification means that Hunters rewards are going away, because the content was too easy and feedback indicated that those rewards were contributing to “trivialization of content”. 

You can read the announcement and FAQ for more on what to expect and what is or isn’t changing for Wuoshi.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/everquest-ii-announces-launch-details-for-next-tle-wuoshi-2000138022
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sky: Children of the Light's Pirate-Themed Event Returns This Year For Treasure Hunt Fun
item.content:encoded:
The multiplatform social adventure MMO Sky: Children of the Light has announced the latest limited-time event, the returning “Days of Treasure,” sending players off on little treasure hunts.  

In this pirate-themed event, players can team up with a team of spirits to search for hidden treasure using a map and earn points for one of two teams, The Scavengers versus The Realm spirits. These quests can be done once a day, and they take place throughout Sky’s many maps. 

Doing the quests will earn players Event Tickets, which can be earned for some nifty pirate-themed rewards, including a cool outfit; as usual, these Event Tickets can also be found around the game for free. 

This limited-time Sky event also runs parallel to the longer “Season of the Carnival,” an overarching series of quests and items with carnival-themed games and missions, even allowing players to make their own game using the Memories features. Plus, Sky is always building out its capabilities, with the “Presence” feature in early stages of launch. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/sky-children-of-the-lights-pirate-themed-event-returns-this-year-for-treasure-hunt-fun-2000138020
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New 'Inside Star Citizen' Video Announces No CitizenCon This Year, Overviews Alpha 4.8 Features
item.content:encoded:
A new “Inside Star Citizen” video gives a general preview of Star Citizen’s Alpha 4.8 and the patches beyond, while offering some other news bits—including the absence of one big event. 

The biggest news takeaway is that there’ll be no CitizenCon this year—neither in-person nor digital. In short, it sounds like they’re taking a note from the Squadron 42 team, which also opted to skip the presentation last year in order to focus on actually getting the game out this year. 

The most notable piece of content coming to Star Citizen for this primary patch is the Strike Groups, which are team-focused PvE missions that focus more heavily on spaceship dogfighting than previous group content. Players are tasked in this case with infiltrating a repurposed and reinforced extraction station by first taking out enemy ships, then either taking down or getting past turrets, before finally freeing the hostages. To top things off, on the way out, a Vanduul Mauler just starts blasting. 

If you’re a good person, or want to try to charge others for your being a good person, Star Citizen will allow hangar services to take place within larger ships. Basically, you can take smaller ships into larger ships with hangars in order to refuel, rearm, or repair them. For those who don’t run into others in the wild, some more refueling missions are coming as well. 

Another big addition coming for three ships is the command center, which allows you to control parts of the ship more individually. Plus, there are more FPS armor, weapons, and more coming. 

In upcoming patches, there are also more Defend missions, Alien week, and the return of the Xeno threat on the way. And down the line in 2026, there are Nyx planets built by the Genesis system, the Siege of Orison, and—they dare say—the spin-off shooter Squadron 42. Yes, they’re quite confident about that coming this year still. 

On top of all of this, DefenseCon is coming next week, which seems to be replacing the Fleet Week that normally happens this time of year.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/new-inside-star-citizen-video-announces-no-citizencon-this-year-overviews-alpha-48-features-2000138018
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Last Epoch Launches a Series of Community Polls to Get Focused Feedback and Consistently Improve the ARPG
item.content:encoded:
Eleventh Hour Games is working to stay on top of the community’s concerns, pain points, and deliver on a high-quality ARPG in Last Epoch. So they’ve just begun a feedback series that is part of an overall effort to strengthen development and draw from the community’s input.

While using community feedback has been ongoing since before launch, this new initiative comes in the form of a poll series. Starting with today's poll, we should see new community polls over the next few weeks, each one focused on a specific player experience aspect of the game. This will get the team focused feedback on specific player experience aspects of the game in addition  to the general feedback they're already getting.

The team released 10 topics that we will be seeing polls on and they are:

  • Skills and Passives
  • Endgame
  • Combat
  • Items
  • UI
  • Audio
  • Connectivity and Performance
  • Environment
  • Quests
  • Crafting

The first poll is on Skills and Passives, with the first question asking which two of four provided options impacts your skills and passives experience the most. The second question asks how often skill or passive issues impact your play experience.

If you want to help them improve Last Epoch, you can go and share your experience in the first poll and continue keeping an eye out for the next ones.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/last-epoch-launches-a-series-of-community-polls-to-get-focused-feedback-and-consistently-improve-the-arpg-2000138021
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Self-Hosted Servers Are Now Deployable In Dune: Awakening's Test Server
item.content:encoded:
The latest Public Test Server for Dune: Awakening lets players finally give self-hosted servers a go, after only allowing paid, officially-sanctioned servers to run at launch. 

The team has discussed some of the capabilities in the past, including custom settings, but now there’s also a quick blog on the requirements for hosting the servers. In short, the technical requirements are, straight from the page: 

  • A Windows machine that has Virtualization and HyperV enabled (Windows Pro is required for this)
  • 20GB of memory, depending on how many servers the battlegroup will have

In the past, they’ve also said the hardware requirements are Windows 10 64-bit Pro with Hyper-V, 20 GB of RAM, 100GB of storage, and a Intel Core i5-8400 or AMD Ryzen 5 1600, along with a mandatory SSD requirement.

The full self-hosted servers page then outlines how you can actually set up the servers, including tokens, port forwarding settings, a more technical explanation of the self-hosting situation and beyond. It’s worth noting that they call the overall server groups “battlegroups” instead of “Worlds” here and the instances “servers” instead of “Sietches.” 

Also included in this ongoing PTS are some of the other 1.4.0 additions and changes previously shared by the Funcom team, more specifically the new Overland locations.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/self-hosted-servers-are-now-deployable-in-dune-awakenings-test-server-2000138019
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
